translation.en: They always see hardship in life.

content.sentence: Họ luôn thấy ___ trong cuộc sống.

notes_md:
**Vất vả** means hard, tiring, exhausting — when something takes a lot of effort and energy. It describes work, tasks, situations, or even a period of life.

It’s an adjective, so you put it after the noun: *công việc vất vả*, *cuộc sống vất vả*. You can also use it after verbs like *rất / quá / khá* to adjust intensity.

It often carries an emotional tone — not just physically hard, but mentally draining too.  

Close synonyms: **cực**, **cực khổ** (stronger, more suffering).  
Opposite idea: **nhàn** (easy, relaxed, not demanding).
exampleSentencesTranslated: His job at the factory is hard work every day.
After a day of hard work, I just want to rest and watch a movie.
Mom did hard work raising us to grow up.

example.en: Life in this city is really tough.
example.es: La vida en esta ciudad es realmente dura.
example.vi: Cuộc sống ở thành phố này thật vất vả.

definitions.en: Difficult and tiring; requiring a lot of effort.
definitions.es: Difícil y agotador; que requiere mucho esfuerzo.
definitions.vi: Khó khăn và mệt mỏi; đòi hỏi nhiều nỗ lực.

notes:
Vất vả means hard, tiring, exhausting — when something takes a lot of effort and energy. It describes work, tasks, situations, or even a period of life.

It’s an adjective, so you put it after the noun: công việc vất vả, cuộc sống vất vả. You can also use it after verbs like rất / quá / khá to adjust intensity.

It often carries an emotional tone — not just physically hard, but mentally draining too.  

Close synonyms: cực, cực khổ (stronger, more suffering).  
Opposite idea: nhàn (easy, relaxed, not demanding).
exampleSentences: Công việc của anh ở nhà máy rất vất vả mỗi ngày.
Sau một ngày vất vả, em chỉ muốn nghỉ ngơi và xem phim.
Mẹ đã vất vả nuôi dạy chúng tôi trưởng thành.
